page: Microsoft Azure F48alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Faldsv7 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor using the x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The server features 48 dedicated physical cores with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-CPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance includes 5669 GB of local disk capacity and supports Premium Storage. No GPU accelerators are present.

In benchmark evaluations, the F48alds_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests. The AMD EPYC processor's high clock speed and dedicated core allocation drive top-tier results in PassMark CPU tests, including physics and extended instructions. Memory performance is strong, with small-block cached read bandwidth reaching the top 10% at 9203.3 GB/sec, while large-block uncached read and write bandwidths rank in the top 25%. Data compression and decompression tasks, such as single-threaded gzip and multi-threaded bzip3, also achieve top-tier throughput.

The server's resource profile makes it highly suitable for compute-intensive workloads that benefit from high clock speeds and dedicated cores but do not require massive memory footprints. It excels in database operations and key-value store transactions, as evidenced by top-tier PassMark database and Redis SET benchmarks. Additionally, it handles small-to-medium LLM inference tasks efficiently, though performance drops to average levels for larger 70B parameter models due to the 96.0 GB memory constraint. This configuration offers high compute density and local storage capacity, making it a cost-efficient choice for high-throughput web serving, batch processing, and computational modeling where memory-to-CPU ratios are kept low.
description: Microsoft Azure F48alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Faldsv7 family, powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, 96.0 GB of memory, and 5669 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server delivers top-tier CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core and multi-core benchmarks. It also demonstrates strong memory bandwidth and top-tier database processing capabilities. Lacking a GPU, it is not designed for hardware-accelerated graphics workloads. The F48alds_v7 is highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy, low-memory tasks, making it an excellent fit for high-throughput web serving, database operations, data compression, and small-to-medium language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F48alds_v7 is a compute-optimized VM featuring a 48-core AMD EPYC processor, 96 GB RAM, and 5.6 TB local storage, delivering top-tier CPU and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F48alds_v7 is a 48-core AMD EPYC compute-optimized VM with 96 GB RAM and 5.6 TB local storage, built for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, dedicated 48-core compute platform with dense local storage for intensive processing and database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 cores operating at 4.5 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio
5669 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier CPU, compression, and database benchmark performance
Suited for compute-heavy workloads, web serving, and database operations
